disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapidly spreading crusted facial sores over 24-48 hours, starting around the nose and extending to the upper lip and cheek
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me what's been happening with your skin?
Where did it start and how has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as tiny blisters by my nose two days ago and now there are crusty yellow sores spreading onto my lip and cheek — it's moving really fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lesions began as small vesicles that ruptured easily and evolved into thick, adherent golden/honey-coloured crusts on an erythematous base
trigger_question_examples: What did the sores look like when they started?
What colour and texture are the crusts?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They were little blisters that burst really easily, and now they've dried into thick yellow-golden crusts stuck on top of red skin.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Onset followed minor mechanical trauma to the nares (repeated nose-blowing) during a mild viral upper respiratory tract infection
trigger_question_examples: Was there anything just before the sores started — a cold, an injury, or skin irritation?
Had you been unwell in any way recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'd had a head cold and was blowing my nose constantly, and the first blisters came up right around my nostrils after that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lesions are non-tender and only mildly pruritic; the patient denies significant pain
trigger_question_examples: Are the sores painful, or more itchy?
Does it hurt when you touch them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're not really sore at all, just a bit itchy — it's the spreading and how it looks that bothers me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Self-treatment with an over-the-counter topical corticosteroid (hydrocortisone) applied to the lesions over the preceding day
trigger_question_examples: Have you put any creams, ointments or medicines on it?
What have you tried on the sores so far?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been dabbing an over-the-counter hydrocortisone cream on them since yesterday, but honestly it seems to have made it worse.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I didn't think a bit of cream would matter — isn't hydrocortisone meant to be safe?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors or systemic toxicity; the patient feels generally well in themselves
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, chills or shakes?
Do you feel unwell in yourself, or just the skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers or shivers, I feel well in myself — it's really just my face.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No mouth ulcers or gum involvement; oral mucosa appears normal to the patient
trigger_question_examples: Are there any sores or ulcers inside your mouth or on your gums?
Any blisters on your lips edge that look different?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the inside of my mouth looks fine — it's all on the outside of my face.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Shares towels, linen and a bathroom with household members; high concern about household transmission
trigger_question_examples: Who else is at home with you?
Do you share towels or bedding with anyone?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live with my family and we all share towels and the bathroom — I'm really worried they'll catch it.

trigger_question_examples: Are you allergic to any medicines, especially antibiotics like penicillin?
Have you ever reacted badly to a medication?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not allergic to any medicines that I know of.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No involvement of or spread toward the eyes; vision normal, no eye pain or swelling
trigger_question_examples: Is any of it near or around your eyes?
Any eye pain, swelling, or change in your vision?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it hasn't gone near my eyes and my vision is completely normal.
